name: impact-stack-rest

metadata.name: impact-stack-rest
metadata.summary: REST API client classes for inter-services communication in Impact Stack.

documentation:
### Impact Stack REST

This library implements utilities for making inter services requests between Impact Stack
microservices.

All the requests are authenticated using JWTs that are previously fetched from the auth-service.

modules.name: impact_stack.rest.utils
documentation: Define various helper functions.

functions.name: impact_stack.rest.utils.class_from_path
documentation: Import a class using its qualified name.

documentation: Define custom exception classes.

documentation: Exception raised for attempts to create forwarding clients from unauthorized requests.

classes.name: impact_stack.rest.rest.Client

methods.name: impact_stack.rest.rest.Client.__init__
documentation: Create a new API client instance.

methods.name: impact_stack.rest.rest.Client.__getattr__
documentation: All undefined attributes are interpreted as HTTP verbs.

methods.name: impact_stack.rest.rest.Client.request
documentation:
Send a request to the API-endpoint and handle the response.

This accepts either of (the first of these wins):
- A url passed as kwarg.
- A path passed as kwarg.
- Path parts that will be escaped and joined passed as positional arguments.

methods.name: impact_stack.rest.rest.Client.__del__
documentation: Close the session.
A General purpose REST / JSON API-client.

modules.name: impact_stack.rest
documentation: Useful wrappers for the requests library.

classes.name: impact_stack.rest.AuthMiddleware

methods.name: impact_stack.rest.AuthMiddleware.__init__
documentation: Create new auth-app requests auth middleware.

methods.name: impact_stack.rest.AuthMiddleware.needs_refresh
documentation: Check if we have a token and it can still be used.

methods.name: impact_stack.rest.AuthMiddleware.get_token
documentation: Use the API key to get a JWT.

methods.name: impact_stack.rest.AuthMiddleware.__call__
documentation: Add the JWT token to the request.

Requests middleware for authenticating using JWT tokens.

The middleware transparently requests an API-token from the auth-app during the first request.
Afterwards each request gets the token added to its headers.

A new token is requested on-demand whenever the old token has (almost) expired.
